title: lupinus latifolius subsp. wigginsii
intro: Lupinus latifolius subsp. wigginsii is a subspecies of the lupine plant, scientifically known as Lupinus latifolius, and is a perennial legume native to California in the United States. It is also known as Wiggins' broad-leaved lupine. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Lupinus latifolius subsp. wigginsii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, which can be achieved through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
